meta.title: Greenpill Network Graph
meta.description: Research-first graph dataset for Greenpill Network with structural relationships and capital flows separated.
notes: Structural relationships are directly evidenced or strongly evidenced.
Capital flows are directional and often inferred from multiple sources.
Workshop-derived nodes are kept but tagged with lower confidence.

capital_taxonomy.label: Knowledge
capital_taxonomy.description: Learning, research, sensemaking, publishing, onboarding material.

capital_taxonomy.label: Social
capital_taxonomy.description: Trust, relationships, introductions, belonging, network cohesion.

capital_taxonomy.label: Financial
capital_taxonomy.description: Funding, treasury flows, grants, streaming capital, revenue.

capital_taxonomy.label: Reputational
capital_taxonomy.description: Credibility, prestige, visibility, endorsements, trust signals.

capital_taxonomy.label: Governance
capital_taxonomy.description: Roles, credentials, accountability, decision rights, permissions.

capital_taxonomy.label: Coordination
capital_taxonomy.description: Operational alignment across people, chapters, tools, and programs.

capital_taxonomy.label: Cultural
capital_taxonomy.description: Shared story, identity, values, aesthetics, local meaning-making.

capital_taxonomy.label: Ecological
capital_taxonomy.description: Biodiversity, local regeneration, living-system value and stewardship.

capital_taxonomy.label: Operational
capital_taxonomy.description: Execution, logistics, stewardship capacity, day-to-day maintenance.

sources.title: Current repo chapter map
sources.note: Current chapter nodes and coordinates used in the checked-out website.

sources.title: Greenpill 2024: A Year in Review
sources.note: Best public recap of chapter activity and local initiatives.

sources.title: Bringing Greenpill.network On-Chain & Proving Out Revenue in 2025
sources.note: Core source for network structure, chapters, guilds, pods, and capital strategy.

sources.title: New Chapter Onboarding
sources.note: Source for chapter steward process, credentials, and support infrastructure.

sources.title: Greenpill Garden - Season One
sources.note: Source for steward roles, service providers, capital forms, and CIDS impact reporting.

sources.title: Greenpill Network Community Call - March 2025
sources.note: Source for chapter demo days, Writers Guild working groups, and Dev Guild builder opportunities.

sources.title: Greenpill Network Community Call - June 2025
sources.note: Source for Greenpill Garden, Writers Guild survey, Cookie Jars, and network restructuring.

sources.title: Greenpill Dev Guild GitHub organization
sources.note: Source for current project nodes and descriptions.

sources.title: GreenPill Podcast on Apple Podcasts
sources.note: Source confirming the podcast as an active public surface.

sources.title: Workshop artifacts supplied in the current conversation
sources.note: Source for exploratory topic pods, audience groups, and adjacent network concepts.

nodes.label: Greenpill Network
nodes.description: Global regenerative network connecting chapters, guilds, programs, media, and tooling.

nodes.label: Network Stewards
nodes.description: Core network stewardship layer responsible for approvals, onboarding, and coordination.

nodes.label: Chapter Stewards
nodes.description: Local chapter leaders who complete onboarding, receive credentials, and run local initiatives.

nodes.label: Greenpill Chapters
nodes.description: Regional chapter layer of the network.

nodes.label: Greenpill Dev Guild
nodes.description: Builder guild producing open-source tools and hosting builder spaces.

nodes.label: Greenpill Writers Guild
nodes.description: Publishing and sensemaking guild contributing writing, surveys, and working groups.

nodes.label: Greenpill Community Calls
nodes.description: Monthly online calls for chapter, guild, program, and ecosystem updates.

nodes.label: Greenpill Garden
nodes.description: Flagship steward program connecting strategy, forms of capital, CIDS reporting, and web3 tooling.

nodes.label: GreenPill Podcast
nodes.description: Podcast surface for education, narrative, and public-facing reach.

nodes.label: Greenpill on Paragraph
nodes.description: Paragraph publication surface for updates, essays, and year-in-review reporting.

nodes.label: Taking the Greenpill
nodes.description: General network onboarding resource linked from the live site.

nodes.label: Taking the DevPill
nodes.description: Builder-focused onboarding pathway referenced in public network discussion.

nodes.label: Greenpill Brasil
nodes.description: Chapter node with public signals around bee traps, agroforestry, and Green Goods pilot activity.

nodes.label: Greenpill Ottawa
nodes.description: Chapter with public signals around sapling donations and cross-chapter aid.

nodes.label: Greenpill Toronto
nodes.description: Chapter with public signals around TCOIN presentation and Great Lakes regional connection.

nodes.label: Greenpill Nigeria
nodes.description: Chapter with public signals around DePin-style solar charging prototype and cleanup activity.

nodes.label: Greenpill Koh Phangan
nodes.description: Chapter with public signals around cleanup events and translated learning resources.

nodes.label: Greenpill Uncommons
nodes.description: China / Uncommons node with public signals around educational translations and commons publishing.

nodes.label: Greenpill CIV
nodes.description: Côte d'Ivoire chapter listed on the current chapter map.

nodes.label: Greenpill Kenya
nodes.description: Kenya chapter listed on the current chapter map.

nodes.label: Greenpill India
nodes.description: India chapter listed on the current chapter map.

nodes.label: Greenpill NYC
nodes.description: NYC chapter listed on the current chapter map and explicitly referenced in steward bios.

nodes.label: Green Goods
nodes.description: PWA for bringing community and environmental actions onchain to measure and reward local impact.

nodes.label: GreenWill
nodes.description: Mini-app for accumulating Greenpill and ReFi reputation.

nodes.label: Impact Reef
nodes.description: Tool for evaluating public-good projects across the regen ecosystem with quality endorsements.

nodes.label: Cookie Jar
nodes.description: Shared ETH/ERC20 funding pool with configurable access and withdrawal rules.

nodes.label: Gardens
nodes.description: Funding and coordination infrastructure used in the Greenpill Garden context.

nodes.label: Greenpill Commons
nodes.description: App for communities to propose local problems and solutions onchain and upvote priorities.

nodes.label: Allo Yeeter
nodes.description: Simple tool for distributing funds in a few steps using Allo Protocol.

nodes.label: Regen Coordination
nodes.description: Coordination surface and ecosystem context repeatedly intertwined with Greenpill strategy and programming.

nodes.label: GitHub
nodes.description: Code and project platform used by the Dev Guild.

nodes.label: Luma
nodes.description: Event platform for community calls and builder spaces.

nodes.label: Discord
nodes.description: Primary community coordination platform referenced in onboarding and participation flows.

nodes.label: Charmverse
nodes.description: Workspace used for chapter pages, applications, resources, and tasks.

nodes.label: Karma GAP
nodes.description: Impact reporting and tech support partner used in Greenpill Garden.

nodes.label: Gitcoin
nodes.description: Public-goods ecosystem anchor repeatedly referenced in Greenpill strategy and steward bios.

nodes.label: Octant
nodes.description: Funding mechanism and public-goods partner surfaced in network calls and strategy threads.

nodes.label: Allo Capital
nodes.description: Research and workshop partner around ecosystem-wide public-goods value capture.

nodes.label: Atlantis
nodes.description: Service provider in Greenpill Garden with impact-related tooling.

nodes.label: Flow State
nodes.description: Service provider in Greenpill Garden and the Dev Guild streaming campaign context.

nodes.label: Grassroot Economics
nodes.description: Service provider in Greenpill Garden via Sarafu Network support.

nodes.label: Public Good Staking
nodes.description: Staking and DeFi infrastructure proposed to create passive funding for steward initiatives.

nodes.label: CIDS Impact Reporting
nodes.description: Common Impact Data Standards aligned impact reporting layer used in Greenpill Garden.

nodes.label: Education Pod
nodes.description: Exploratory topic pod centered on education, onboarding, and learning cross-pollination.

nodes.label: Environment Pod
nodes.description: Exploratory topic pod around environmental and local regenerative initiatives.

nodes.label: DeSci Pod
nodes.description: Exploratory topic pod around decentralized science and related communities.

nodes.label: DePin Pod
nodes.description: Exploratory topic pod around decentralized physical infrastructure and local deployments.

nodes.label: Kevin Owocki
nodes.description: Public founder figure and media host strongly associated with Greenpill and its podcast surface.

nodes.label: Afolabi Aiyeloja
nodes.description: Core steward of Greenpill Network and Dev Guild with active involvement in Gardens, Regen Coordination, and Gitcoin.

nodes.label: Matt Strachman
nodes.description: Steward of the Greenpill Network, NYC Chapter, Writers Guild, and Dev Guild treasury work.

nodes.label: Local Organizers
nodes.description: People exploring whether to join, start, or steward local Greenpill activity.

nodes.label: Builders
nodes.description: Developers, product builders, and technical contributors entering through the Dev Guild.

nodes.label: Writers and Educators
nodes.description: Writers, educators, researchers, and sensemakers entering through media and publishing surfaces.

nodes.label: Partners and Funders
nodes.description: External organizations evaluating Greenpill for grants, pilots, and collaboration.

nodes.label: Local Communities
nodes.description: Community members reached by local chapter initiatives and events.

relationships.notes: Network stewards review applications and manage onboarding.
Chapter steward role is a formal part of onboarding and credentials.
Chapters are one of the network's main branches.

The monthly call is branded as a Greenpill Network call.
Greenpill Garden is presented as a network steward season.
Podcast is one of the main public media surfaces.
Paragraph functions as a public update and publication channel.
The live site links to Taking the Greenpill.
Taking the DevPill is referenced in public network discussion.
Discord is a primary community coordination surface.
Charmverse is used for applications, pages, resources, and tasks.
Luma is the current event platform for network calls.
Greenpill strategy and Garden programming are deeply intertwined with Regen Coordination.

capital_flows.mechanism: Episodes, guest conversations, public-facing narrative surface
capital_flows.notes: The podcast helps shape public identity and bring attention into the network.

capital_flows.mechanism: Education and inspiration for newcomers
capital_flows.notes: The podcast is presented as an educational entrypoint.

capital_flows.mechanism: Year-in-review posts, updates, essays, and chapter reporting
capital_flows.notes: Paragraph produces public narrative memory and evidence of activity.

capital_flows.mechanism: Publishing surface and circulation channel
capital_flows.notes: Paragraph gives writers a visible output surface.

capital_flows.mechanism: Writing, reporting, surveys, working groups
capital_flows.notes: The Writers Guild increases narrative coherence and documentation.

capital_flows.mechanism: Chapter storytelling, updates, and public reporting
capital_flows.notes: Chapter visibility improves when stories and outcomes are written up.

capital_flows.mechanism: Monthly updates, open discussion, working-group alignment
capital_flows.notes: The calls are explicitly for updates, discussion, and network alignment.

capital_flows.mechanism: Chapter updates, demo days, and shared discussion
capital_flows.notes: Chapter updates and demo days are part of public call agendas.

capital_flows.mechanism: Guild updates and builder opportunity sharing
capital_flows.notes: Dev Guild surfaces opportunities through network calls.

capital_flows.mechanism: Writers Guild survey and working-group coordination
capital_flows.notes: Calls are directly used to coordinate Writers Guild activity.

capital_flows.mechanism: Local initiatives, chapter presence, regional experimentation
capital_flows.notes: Chapters bring local proof, local identity, and real-world relevance into the network.

capital_flows.mechanism: Bee conservation, agroforestry, community living center, Green Goods pilot
capital_flows.notes: Brasil contributes some of the clearest local-regeneration proof points.

capital_flows.mechanism: Sapling donations and inter-chapter solidarity
capital_flows.notes: Ottawa adds visible local stewardship and cross-chapter support.

capital_flows.mechanism: TCOIN presentation and chapter-level public programming
capital_flows.notes: Toronto contributes public convening and knowledge exchange.

capital_flows.mechanism: Solar charging prototype and local impact cleanup
capital_flows.notes: Nigeria demonstrates Greenpill's DePin and local-infrastructure potential.

capital_flows.mechanism: Cleanup day and translated learning resources
capital_flows.notes: Koh Phangan contributes ecological action and translation-based knowledge circulation.

capital_flows.mechanism: Chinese translations, co-study, commons publishing
capital_flows.notes: Uncommons is a strong knowledge commons and translation node.

capital_flows.mechanism: Builder Spaces, open-source tools, tech support
capital_flows.notes: The Dev Guild is a major technical and implementation engine for the network.

capital_flows.mechanism: Builder Spaces, public repos, open contribution pathways
capital_flows.notes: The Dev Guild gives builders skill development, social proximity, and proof-of-work surfaces.

capital_flows.mechanism: Tech support, educational content, workshops
capital_flows.notes: The Greenpill Garden source explicitly lists Dev Guild support to stewards.

capital_flows.mechanism: Onchain tracking and rewarding of community and environmental actions
capital_flows.notes: Green Goods is designed to support local impact measurement and reward flows.

capital_flows.mechanism: Reputation accumulation and allowlist or credential logic
capital_flows.notes: GreenWill appears as the network's clearest reputation layer.

capital_flows.mechanism: Project evaluation and quality endorsements
capital_flows.notes: Impact Reef helps funders and evaluators reason about public-good quality.

capital_flows.mechanism: Shared funding pools with configurable access rules
capital_flows.notes: Cookie Jar directly contributes treasury tooling for communities.

capital_flows.mechanism: Funding pool and program coordination infrastructure
capital_flows.notes: Gardens appears in network calls as shared funding infrastructure.

capital_flows.mechanism: Fast distribution of funds using Allo Protocol
capital_flows.notes: Allo Yeeter contributes capital distribution capacity.

capital_flows.mechanism: Forum, ecosystem alignment, educational content, CIDS implementation support
capital_flows.notes: Regen Coordination acts as connective infrastructure for Greenpill.

capital_flows.mechanism: Public-goods legitimacy, grants context, ecosystem proximity
capital_flows.notes: Gitcoin appears in steward bios, ecosystem updates, and Greenpill's broader positioning.

capital_flows.mechanism: Public-goods round participation and ecosystem validation
capital_flows.notes: Octant appears as a funding mechanism in Greenpill's ecosystem context.

capital_flows.mechanism: Research, workshops, and public-goods value-capture thinking
capital_flows.notes: Allo Capital contributes capital-allocation research and workshop context.

capital_flows.mechanism: Theory of change, structures, protocols, workshops, role development
capital_flows.notes: This is one of the clearest direct capital-transfer relationships in the source set.

capital_flows.mechanism: Refined identity, stronger local infrastructures, impact reporting, shared learning
capital_flows.notes: The proposal explicitly frames Garden outcomes as network-level value.

capital_flows.mechanism: CIDS reporting support and impact record infrastructure
capital_flows.notes: Karma GAP directly supports steward impact reporting.

capital_flows.mechanism: Flow councils and streaming support
capital_flows.notes: Flow State is listed as a Greenpill Garden support provider.

capital_flows.mechanism: Impact tooling and tech support
capital_flows.notes: Atlantis is directly named as an impact-related service provider.

capital_flows.mechanism: Sarafu Network support and community-currency know-how
capital_flows.notes: Grassroot Economics contributes alternative economic infrastructure and know-how.

capital_flows.mechanism: Passive funding infrastructure for steward initiatives
capital_flows.notes: Public Good Staking is explicitly framed as a funding mechanism.

capital_flows.mechanism: Shared standards for activities, outcomes, and impact reporting
capital_flows.notes: CIDS turns program activity into legible, reportable impact.

capital_flows.mechanism: General onboarding and educational pathway
capital_flows.notes: This pathway functions as a narrative and knowledge gateway.

capital_flows.mechanism: Builder-specific onboarding pathway
capital_flows.notes: This pathway is a strong candidate to become a structured builder funnel.

capital_flows.mechanism: Founder narrative, books, podcast, public association
capital_flows.notes: Kevin remains a major narrative and credibility node in the public footprint.

capital_flows.mechanism: Core stewardship, program design, builder and ecosystem bridge work
capital_flows.notes: Afolabi's steward bio directly describes cross-network and tool coordination work.

capital_flows.mechanism: Network stewardship, Writers Guild support, treasury management, chapter work
capital_flows.notes: Matt's public steward bio ties together multiple network functions.

capital_flows.mechanism: Topic-specific collaboration and ecosystem mapping
capital_flows.notes: Exploratory pod concept from workshop materials.

capital_flows.mechanism: Cross-pollination of onboarding, workshops, and learning resources
capital_flows.notes: Exploratory pod concept from workshop materials.

capital_flows.mechanism: Association with DeSci communities, IP and science coordination
capital_flows.notes: Exploratory pod concept from workshop materials.

capital_flows.mechanism: Local infrastructure pilots and capital formation around real-world deployments
capital_flows.notes: Exploratory pod concept from workshop materials.

capital_flows.mechanism: Local meetups, community events, environmental actions, educational programs
capital_flows.notes: Chapter activities repeatedly describe benefits delivered to local communities.
